title: Cloud & AI Security Engineer Associate (SC-500)
subtitle: Secure Cloud Infrastructure and AI Deployments
description: Learn to secure cloud infrastructure and AI deployments, covering threat modelling for AI, Azure OpenAI security, content filtering, AI governance frameworks, and security operations with Defender XDR and Sentinel
overview: This intermediate course prepares you for the SC-500 certification exam by covering the intersection of cloud security and AI security. You will learn to apply traditional cloud security foundations -- identity, endpoint protection, and SIEM/SOAR -- alongside emerging AI-specific challenges such as AI model threat modelling, securing Azure OpenAI deployments, configuring content filtering, classifying data for AI pipelines, and implementing AI governance frameworks aligned with the Microsoft Responsible AI standard. The course combines defensive security operations with proactive AI risk management.

learningObjectives.title: Cloud Security Foundations
learningObjectives.description: Implement identity and endpoint security, configure conditional access, and manage security posture across Azure and Microsoft 365 environments

learningObjectives.title: AI Security & Threat Modelling
learningObjectives.description: Perform threat modelling for AI systems, secure Azure OpenAI deployments, configure content filtering, and defend against prompt injection and data exfiltration

learningObjectives.title: Data Protection & AI Governance
learningObjectives.description: Classify and protect data flowing through AI pipelines, implement AI governance frameworks, and apply the Microsoft Responsible AI standard

learningObjectives.title: Security Operations & Incident Response
learningObjectives.description: Operate Defender XDR and Microsoft Sentinel for threat detection, investigation, and automated response across cloud and AI workloads

modules.title: Cloud Security Foundations
modules.description: Build the security baseline for cloud environments with identity, endpoint, and posture management
topics: Microsoft Entra ID: authentication, conditional access, and PIM
Endpoint security with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
Network security: NSGs, Azure Firewall, and private endpoints
Microsoft Defender for Cloud: security posture and recommendations
Identity governance and entitlement management
Zero Trust architecture principles applied to Azure
Secure DevOps practices: scanning, secrets management, and supply chain
Compliance frameworks and regulatory alignment in Azure

modules.title: AI Security & Threat Modelling
modules.description: Identify and mitigate security threats specific to AI systems and large language model deployments
topics: AI threat landscape: prompt injection, jailbreaking, and data poisoning
Threat modelling frameworks for AI systems (STRIDE applied to AI)
Securing Azure OpenAI Service deployments: networking, keys, and managed identity
Content filtering configuration: built-in and custom categories
Custom blocklists and output filtering for sensitive content
Defending against indirect prompt injection attacks
Model access controls and deployment-level permissions
Red-teaming AI systems: methodology and tooling

modules.title: Data Protection & AI Governance
modules.description: Protect data in AI pipelines and implement governance frameworks for responsible AI adoption
topics: Data classification for AI training data and RAG knowledge bases
Microsoft Purview for AI data governance and lineage
Sensitivity labels applied to AI pipeline data sources
Data loss prevention policies for AI-generated content
AI governance frameworks and organisational policies
Microsoft Responsible AI standard: fairness, reliability, safety, privacy
AI impact assessments and risk documentation
Regulatory considerations: EU AI Act, GDPR implications for AI

modules.title: Security Operations & Incident Response
modules.description: Detect, investigate, and respond to threats across cloud and AI workloads using Defender XDR and Sentinel
topics: Microsoft Defender XDR: unified threat detection and investigation
Microsoft Sentinel: workspace setup, data connectors, and analytics rules
KQL queries for security investigation and hunting
Automated response with Sentinel playbooks and SOAR capabilities
Incident management workflows and escalation procedures
Threat intelligence integration and indicator management
Monitoring AI-specific security events and anomalies
Security operations for AI workloads: logging, alerting, and forensics

handsOnLabs.title: Identity & Conditional Access Configuration
handsOnLabs.description: Configure conditional access policies, PIM roles, and identity governance for an Azure environment

handsOnLabs.title: AI Threat Modelling Workshop
handsOnLabs.description: Perform threat modelling on an AI application using STRIDE, identify attack vectors, and design mitigations

handsOnLabs.title: Azure OpenAI Content Filtering
handsOnLabs.description: Configure content filtering categories, custom blocklists, and test against adversarial prompts

handsOnLabs.title: Sentinel Threat Investigation
handsOnLabs.description: Set up Sentinel analytics rules, investigate a simulated security incident, and build an automated response playbook

prerequisites: Familiarity with Azure security services and Microsoft Defender products
Understanding of identity and access management concepts
Basic knowledge of AI services and large language models
targetAudience: Security engineers responsible for cloud and AI environments
Cloud architects designing secure AI solutions

instructor.name: Yair Knijn
instructor.title: Principal Cloud Architect & Engineer
instructor.bio: Microsoft Certified Trainer with deep expertise in cloud security and emerging AI security challenges. Experienced in securing Azure OpenAI deployments, implementing AI governance frameworks, and operating Microsoft Sentinel and Defender XDR for enterprise organisations.
certifications: Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate
Microsoft Certified: Security Operations Analyst Associate

certification.name: Microsoft Certified: Cloud and AI Security Engineer Associate

personalStory.story: Security has always been part of cloud work, but AI has introduced threats that most security teams have never seen before. Prompt injection is not in any traditional playbook. Data exfiltration through a chatbot is not something your firewall rules will catch. I built this course after spending months hardening Azure OpenAI deployments for clients who were rolling out AI features without realising how exposed they were. The good news is that the security fundamentals still apply -- you just need to extend them to cover this new surface area.
